content:
# Microporosity

**Summary**: Pore space with dimensions smaller than 62.5 microns.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013]] analyzed microporosity to understand its impact on permeability and reservoir performance.

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] used MICP to determine the pore aperture size distribution for 10 selected samples.

- [[2015-al-amri|Al-Amri, 2015]] identified microporosity using sonic logs to distinguish low permeability rocks from high quality ones.

- [[2019-alghamdi|AlGhamdi, 2019]] not used

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] identified microporosity as the most predominant pore type (44%) in all lithofacies.

- [[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]] Referenced in this thesis.

## Scientific Context

- [[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013]] Crucial for understanding flow properties and capillary forces in tight carbonate reservoirs.

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] used MICP to determine the pore aperture size distribution for 10 selected samples.

- [[2015-al-amri|Al-Amri, 2015]] identified microporosity using sonic logs to distinguish low permeability rocks from high quality ones.

- [[2019-alghamdi|AlGhamdi, 2019]] not used

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] identified microporosity as the most predominant pore type (44%) in all lithofacies.

- [[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]] Referenced in this thesis.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013]] difference between core-plug porosity and point-count porosity from thin sections

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Microporosity in the samples ranged from 0.4% to 6.4%. ([[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013, p. 18]])


- The analysis was conducted using the Micromeritics AutoPore IV 9500 high-pressure mercury porosimeter. ([[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014, p. 38]])

- Lithofacies 4 showed a significant difference between sonic and density-neutron porosity, indicating the presence of microporosity. ([[2015-al-amri|Al-Amri, 2015, p. 67]])

- N/A ([[2019-alghamdi|AlGhamdi, 2019]])

- Microporosity is primarily inter-micrite crystals and accounts for 44% of total porosity across all samples. ([[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020, p. 137]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]])
